 {"id":"bd-1dez.6","title":"bd mol publish: Push formula to GitHub repo","description":"Publish a formula to a GitHub repository for sharing.

## Usage
```bash
# Create new repo and publish
bd mol publish mol-my-workflow --repo github.com/myorg/mol-my-workflow

# Update existing repo
bd mol publish mol-my-workflow --repo github.com/myorg/mol-my-workflow --tag v1.1.0
```

## Implementation
1. Export proto to formula.json (if not already a file)
2. Validate formula structure
3. Create GitHub repo if --create flag (uses `gh repo create`)
4. Copy formula.json to repo
5. Generate README.md from formula description
6. Commit and push
7. Create git tag if --tag specified
8. Add mol-formula topic to repo

## Flags
- `--repo <url>` - Target GitHub repo (required)
- `--tag <version>` - Create version tag
- `--create` - Create repo if doesn't exist
- `--private` - Create as private repo

## Workflow
```bash
# Full publish flow
bd mol distill my-epic --name my-workflow
bd mol promote bd-proto-xxx --as my-workflow
bd mol publish mol-my-workflow --repo github.com/me/mol-my-workflow --create --tag v1.0.0
```

 {"id":"bd-1dez.7","title":"Formula versioning: Version field and git tag integration","description":"Add versioning support to formulas for tracking updates.

## Formula Version Field
```json
{
  "formula": "mol-code-review",
  "version": "1.2.0",
  "min_bd_version": "0.25.0",
  ...
}
```

## Version Semantics
- Use semver: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH
- MAJOR: Breaking changes to step structure
- MINOR: New optional steps or variables
- PATCH: Documentation, bug fixes

## Git Tag Integration
- `bd mol install repo@v1.2.0` fetches that tag
- `bd mol install repo` fetches latest tag (or main if no tags)
- `bd mol publish --tag v1.2.0` creates tag

## Proto Version Tracking
Local proto should store:
```
source_repo: github.com/anthropics/mol-code-review
source_version: v1.2.0
installed_at: 2025-12-25T12:00:00Z
```

This enables `bd mol update` to check for newer versions.

 {"id":"bd-1dez.8","title":"Installation tracking: .beads/installed.json","description":"Track installed formulas for update checking and provenance.

## File Format
```json
{
  "mol-code-review": {
    "source": "github.com/anthropics/mol-code-review",
    "version": "v1.2.0",
    "installed_at": "2025-12-25T12:00:00Z",
    "formula_hash": "abc123..."
  },
  "mol-polecat-work": {
    "source": "local",
    "version": "1",
    "installed_at": "2025-12-24T10:00:00Z"
  }
}
```

## Commands That Update This
- `bd mol install` - Adds entry
- `bd mol update` - Updates version/timestamp
- `bd cook` - Adds entry with source:local
- `bd mol uninstall` - Removes entry (new command)

## Usage
```bash
bd mol list --installed    # Shows installed with source/version
bd mol outdated            # Shows formulas with available updates
```

## Sync Behavior
- installed.json is gitignored (local state)
- Only formulas/ directory is synced
- Each clone tracks its own installations

 {"id":"bd-m964","title":"Consider FTS5 for text search at scale","description":"SearchIssues uses LIKE patterns for text search which can't use indexes.

**Current query (queries.go:1475-1477):**
```sql
(title LIKE ? OR description LIKE ? OR id LIKE ?)
```

**Problem:** Full table scan on every text search. At 100K+ issues, this becomes slow.

**SQLite FTS5 solution:**
```sql
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE issues_fts USING fts5(
  id, title, description, design, notes,
  content='issues',
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

-- Triggers to keep FTS in sync
CREATE TRIGGER issues_ai AFTER INSERT ON issues BEGIN
  INSERT INTO issues_fts(rowid, id, title, description, design, notes)
  VALUES (new.rowid, new.id, new.title, new.description, new.design, new.notes);
END;
-- (similar for UPDATE, DELETE)
```

**Trade-offs:**
- Database size increase (~30-50% for text content)
- Additional write overhead (trigger execution)
- Better search capabilities (ranking, phrase search)

**Decision needed:** Is full-text search a priority feature? Current LIKE search may be acceptable for most use cases.

**Benchmark first:** Measure SearchIssues at 100K scale before implementing.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:56.466121-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:56.466121-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m964","depends_on_id":"bd-h0we","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:56.466764-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

-{"id":"bd-mol-xga","title":"mol-beads-release","description":"Release checklist for beads version 0.37.0.

This molecule ensures all release steps are completed properly.
Variable: 0.37.0 - target version (e.g., 0.35.0)

## Step: update-release-notes
Update cmd/bd/info.go with release notes for 0.37.0.

Add a new VersionChange entry at the top of versionChanges slice:
```go
{
    Version: "0.37.0",
    Date:    "YYYY-MM-DD",
    Changes: []string{
        "NEW: Feature description",
        "FIX: Bug fix description",
        "IMPROVED: Enhancement description",
    },
},
```

Run `git log --oneline v<previous>..HEAD` to see what changed.

## Step: update-changelog
Update CHANGELOG.md with detailed release notes.

Add a new section after [Unreleased]:
```markdown
## [0.37.0] - YYYY-MM-DD

### Added
- **Feature name** (issue-id) - Description

### Changed
- **Change description** (issue-id)

### Fixed
- **Bug fix** (issue-id) - Description
```

Sort by importance, not chronologically.
Needs: update-release-notes

## Step: bump-version
Run the version bump script.

```bash
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.37.0
```

This updates version in all files:
- cmd/bd/version.go
- .claude-plugin/*.json
- integrations/beads-mcp/pyproject.toml
- npm-package/package.json
- Hook templates

Needs: update-changelog

## Step: run-tests
Run tests and verify lint passes.

```bash
go test -short ./...
```

CI will run full lint, but fix any obvious issues first.
Needs: bump-version

## Step: commit-release
Commit the release changes.

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: bump version to v0.37.0"
```

Needs: run-tests

## Step: push-and-tag
Push commit and create release tag.

```bash
git push origin main
git tag v0.37.0
git push origin v0.37.0
```

This triggers GitHub Actions release workflow.
Needs: commit-release

## Step: wait-for-ci
Wait for GitHub Actions to complete.

Monitor: https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/actions

CI will:
- Build binaries via GoReleaser
- Create GitHub Release with assets
- Publish to npm (@beads/bd)
- Publish to PyPI (beads-mcp)
- Update Homebrew tap

Wait until all jobs succeed (~5-10 min).
Needs: push-and-tag

## Step: verify-release
Verify the release is complete.

```bash
# Check GitHub release
gh release view v0.37.0

# Check Homebrew
brew update && brew info steveyegge/beads/bd

# Check npm
npm view @beads/bd version

# Check PyPI
pip index versions beads-mcp
```

Needs: wait-for-ci

## Step: update-local
Update local installations.

```bash
# Upgrade Homebrew
brew upgrade steveyegge/beads/bd

# Or install from source
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.37.0 --install

# Install MCP locally
pip install -e integrations/beads-mcp

# Restart daemons
pkill -f "bd daemon" || true
```

Verify: `bd --version` shows 0.37.0
Needs: verify-release

## Step: manual-publish
(Optional) Manual publish if CI failed.

```bash
# npm (requires npm login)
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.37.0 --publish-npm

# PyPI (requires TWINE credentials)
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.37.0 --publish-pypi

# Or both
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.37.0 --publish-all
```

Only needed if CI publishing failed.

Variable: 0.37.0 - target version (e.g., 0.35.0)

## Step: update-release-notes
Update cmd/bd/info.go with release notes for 0.37.0.

Add a new VersionChange entry at the top of versionChanges slice:
```go
{
    Version: "0.37.0",
    Date:    "YYYY-MM-DD",
    Changes: []string{
        "NEW: Feature description",
        "FIX: Bug fix description",
        "IMPROVED: Enhancement description",
    },
},
```

Run `git log --oneline v<previous>..HEAD` to see what changed.

## Step: update-changelog
Update CHANGELOG.md with detailed release notes.

Add a new section after [Unreleased]:
```markdown
## [0.37.0] - YYYY-MM-DD

### Added
- **Feature name** (issue-id) - Description

### Changed
- **Change description** (issue-id)

### Fixed
- **Bug fix** (issue-id) - Description
```

Sort by importance, not chronologically.
Needs: update-release-notes

## Step: bump-version
Run the version bump script.

```bash
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.37.0
```

This updates version in all files:
- cmd/bd/version.go
- .claude-plugin/*.json
- integrations/beads-mcp/pyproject.toml
- npm-package/package.json
- Hook templates

Needs: update-changelog

## Step: run-tests
Run tests and verify lint passes.

```bash
go test -short ./...
```

CI will run full lint, but fix any obvious issues first.
Needs: bump-version

## Step: commit-release
Commit the release changes.

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: bump version to v0.37.0"
```

Needs: run-tests

## Step: push-and-tag
Push commit and create release tag.

```bash
git push origin main
git tag v0.37.0
git push origin v0.37.0
```

This triggers GitHub Actions release workflow.
Needs: commit-release

## Step: wait-for-ci
Wait for GitHub Actions to complete.

Monitor: https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/actions

CI will:
- Build binaries via GoReleaser
- Create GitHub Release with assets
- Publish to npm (@beads/bd)
- Publish to PyPI (beads-mcp)
- Update Homebrew tap

Wait until all jobs succeed (~5-10 min).
Needs: push-and-tag

## Step: verify-release
Verify the release is complete.

```bash
# Check GitHub release
gh release view v0.37.0

# Check Homebrew
brew update && brew info steveyegge/beads/bd

# Check npm
npm view @beads/bd version

# Check PyPI
pip index versions beads-mcp
```

Needs: wait-for-ci

## Step: update-local
Update local installations.

```bash
# Upgrade Homebrew
brew upgrade steveyegge/beads/bd

# Or install from source
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.37.0 --install

# Install MCP locally
pip install -e integrations/beads-mcp

# Restart daemons
pkill -f "bd daemon" || true
```

Verify: `bd --version` shows 0.37.0
Needs: verify-release

## Step: manual-publish
(Optional) Manual publish if CI failed.

```bash
# npm (requires npm login)
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.37.0 --publish-npm

# PyPI (requires TWINE credentials)
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.37.0 --publish-pypi

# Or both
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.37.0 --publish-all
```

Only needed if CI publishing failed.

 {"id":"bd-r2n1","title":"Add integration tests for RPC server and event loops","description":"After adding basic unit tests for daemon utilities, the complex daemon functions still need integration tests:

Core daemon lifecycle:
- startRPCServer: Initializes and starts RPC server with proper error handling
- runEventLoop: Polling-based sync loop with parent monitoring and signal handling
- runDaemonLoop: Main daemon initialization and setup

Health checking:
- isDaemonHealthy: Checks daemon responsiveness and health metrics
- checkDaemonHealth: Periodic health verification

These require more complex test infrastructure (mock RPC, test contexts, signal handling) and should be tackled after the unit test foundation is in place.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T12:28:56.022996362-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T12:44:32.167862713-07:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T12:44:32.167862713-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-r2n1","depends_on_id":"bd-4or","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-18T12:28:56.045893852-07:00","created_by":"mhwilkie"}]}
